name: Q305330
abstract: Ray Bauduc (June 18, 1906 – January 8, 1988) was an American jazz drummer best known for his work with the Bob Crosby Orchestra and their band-within-a-band, the Bobcats, between 1935 and 1942. He is also known for his shared composition of "Big Noise from Winnetka," a jazz standard.

interviews.title: Ray Bauduc and Nappy Lamare Interview, 1959-09-09

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: We were in Chicago with the Bob Crosby band and the drummer was Ray Bauduc.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Allen
about_quotes.evidence: the Bob Crosby Dixieland Band... a fellow named Ray Bauduc B-A-U-D-U-C was the drummer
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Allen interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Hayes Pillars
about_quotes.evidence: I remember the Jack Teagarden was playing in the band, another Teagarden was playing trumpet Ray Bauduc was playing drums... That band -- that was one of the hottest bands, one of the greatest bands that I ever heard.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: jam session sequence featuring Art Tatum piano, Charlie Barnet, alto sax, Ziggy Elman, trumpet, Tommy Dorsey, trombone, Jimmy Dorsey, clarinet, Ray Bauduc, drums

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Bennie Pollack's band with the original band with Ray Bauduc on drums at the Pier -- I had the chance to watch these people in action.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: Ray Bauduc, guys like that, even Davey who went with he can play the Tough when Artie Shaw, was playing four.
